title: dendrosenecio johnstonii subsp. cheranganiensis
intro: Dendrosenecio johnstonii subsp. cheranganiensis, also known as Cherangani giant senecio, is a rare and endangered plant species native to Kenya, belonging to the daisy family and characterized by its large, rosette-shaped leaves and tall, woody stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which are attributed to its alkaloid content. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and has been valued for its potential to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Dendrosenecio johnstonii subsp. cheranganiensis are often used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are believed to offer a range of health benefits.
